name: FlowForge v1.1.0 Release Readiness
summary: Tracks readiness for agency/autonomy evidence, media proofs, Windows repair proof, CLI design language, release gates, and collateral. GATING DECISION (operator, 2026-06-10): the release's marketing story is the agent-behavior improvements, so behavioral confidence must be extremely high — the FULL brittle-phrase/behavioral-confidence sweep blocks the ship: code freeze at eae78ee5 was deliberately broken after manual exercise surfaced guard misfires and canned-prose-in-prod; the release now gates on the complete sweep (evidence-delivery projection, retiring the keyword intent classifier, grounding all ranked behavioral phrase heuristics, adversarial cases for every live finding, full gate re-run, honest-soak remediation of every scenario the canned-path removal exposes, and the git-history personal-content scrub), not on a partial fix set. CEREMONY PREFLIGHT (2026-06-10): a fresh full behavioral soak on the release HEAD surfaced one more guard over-fire (B10/R-BPS-9): ExecutionTruthGuard force-failed a continuation/correction turn that truthfully RECALLED a prior in-session inspection ('name one file you observed') into an ErrGuardExhausted panic, because the recall made no fresh in-turn tool call. Fixed by grounding the inspection-evidence obligation in turn provenance (recallsPriorObservationTurn, gated on prior assistant context so first-turn fabrication is still caught). Authoritative pre-ship soak on the fixed tree (git_commit d288abd1, cloud-provider-v4-flash, cloud-only per directive): 37/38, 0 release-gate findings, 0 retry waits; correction_semantics now grade A. The sole miss is geopolitical_sitrep — live Wikipedia Current-Events source returned no-extract that instant — re-verified PASS in isolation (live-source variance, not a regression). REMEDIATION COMPLETE (2026-06-10 late): the 37/38 run's residual misses were NOT model variance — recorded turn_diagnostic events proved a cascade of guard/judge/routing defects against recall/continuation turns (B11-B13): content-less web fetches counted as grounding evidence; task_deferral and non_repetition rejected user-requested restatement (recalled filename, named topic anchor); named-topic anchor matching missed the model's U+2011 hyphen rendering; the blocked-impact contract lacked the positive 'did change' token form; the fuzzy scheduling lean misrouted return-to-topic turns; and the grounding judge's 6s timeout (two recorded 'context deadline exceeded' failures) converted over-fires into panics (default now 15s). All fixed with regression locks R-BPS-10/11/12 on commit 1ea584bc. Authoritative pre-ship soak on that commit: 38/38, all 23 families grade A, 0 quality slices below floor, 0 release-gate findings, 0 retry waits — the first fully clean suite on release code. Determinism evidence: correction_semantics 10/10 consecutive isolated PASS, continuity_after_blocked_execution 8/8, geopolitical_sitrep 3/3. Full gates green on the same commit (the unit-test suite 60 ok/0 fail, race, lint 0 issues, vet, linux+windows cross-compile, live smoke). CEREMONY EXECUTION (2026-06-11): PR #90 (release->develop) was the branch's first Linux/Windows CI exercise and surfaced five platform defects + a stdlib vuln, each diagnosed from recorded job logs and fixed at the proven cause (artifact-evaluator cold-GOCACHE timeout, Windows monotonic-clock deadline race, Windows 8.3 tilde over-match, non-atomic test config writes double-firing the mtime-poll watcher, WAL busy_timeout 5s->30s, go 1.26.3->1.26.4); CI fully green on round 2. The post-fix soak then surfaced one more behavioral gap (B14/R-BPS-13): a web-read final ending by ANNOUNCING retrieval it never performed (promissory tail, incomplete turn) passed all guards; ExecutionTruthGuard now rejects promissory-retrieval tails with retry. Final authoritative pre-ship soak on release HEAD 8761b3b2: 38/38, all 23 families grade A, 0 release-gate findings; geopolitical_sitrep 6/6 isolated post-fix. CEREMONY COMPLETE (2026-06-11): merged via PR #90 (develop, da4c3d43) and PR #91 (main, 593b9672); tag v1.1.0 pushed after main's exact-SHA CI went green. First release-workflow run died on an upstream 429 fetching the Piper asset; re-dispatched against the existing tag per runbook item 7 — all 5 platform builds, publish, and self-evaluation green. Published release verified live: 18 assets incl. SHA256SUMS (checksum spot-verified), binary reports 1.1.0 on go1.26.4, /releases/latest resolves FlowForge v1.1.0 (displacing the foreign Architext release as Latest). Site release-sync dispatch succeeded; the site's Vercel deploy remains broken by an EXPIRED VERCEL_TOKEN (set 2026-02-23; deploys worked 2026-05-19, identical failure recorded 2026-06-07 pre-ceremony — pre-existing). VERCEL_ORG_ID/VERCEL_PROJECT_ID secrets corrected to API ground truth; token rotation is operator-only (Vercel dashboard), after which deploy.yml re-dispatch publishes the synced v1.1.0 site data.
targetWindow: Release ceremony after the full behavioral-confidence sweep and re-run gates pass
updateSource: v1.1.0 SHIPPED 2026-06-11: tag v1.1.0 on 593b9672, release published + verified live; open follow-ups: site VERCEL_TOKEN rotation (operator), foreign Architext v1.1.2 release cleanup (delegated session)

required.title: Agency/autonomy live execution proof
required.summary: Live hydrated daemon proof now drives one agency-approved candidate into the canonical pipeline and records a terminal execution outcome, and the repeatable agency/autonomy drill harness now passes isolated fresh, seeded-candidate, zero-budget hydrated, and budgeted hydrated variants. The fixes prove websocket runtime apply, daemon agency service/cadence rebind, effective isolated config writes, scheduler wake-up, provider counts, policy disposition, durable agency decision evidence, cloud-token budget enforcement, execution fan-out bounds, and canonical execution handoff. Sustained normal-surface observability and useful autonomous-work completion remain tracked separately as release evidence items, not as incomplete implementation seams.

required.title: Practical the cloud provider media gate
required.summary: Web audio/TTS passed on the cloud provider. Web image passes through normal WebSocket media ingress, runtime-owned vision preprocessing, and final the cloud provider response. Chat Connector voice-note proof reaches central media ingress and STT. Chat Connector photo reaches central image evidence, vision preprocessing, and final the cloud provider response. Spoken weather, fresh external-info, practical flight lookup, correction, CAPTCHA, Piper startup, selected web-read alternate exhaustion, context pressure, provider tool-choice behavior, text-form functionCall normalization, and status-only transport evidence defects are now fixed centrally with focused regression evidence. Normal-surface rerun evidence remains tracked separately as release validation, not as an incomplete media implementation item.
required.rationale: The latest the cloud provider Web audio/TTS rerun passed through the normal Web path. The image leg originally exposed the cloud provider rejecting image_url content, then verifier retry erasure and media-cache replay defects. Runtime-owned vision preprocessing, retry preservation, and media cache bypass now allow the normal Web surface to pass visual proof through final route cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-flash. The first Chat Connector voice-note proof reached central media ingress but failed before pipeline execution because channel-native Ogg/Opus audio was not normalized before whisper.cpp STT. The next Chat Connector proof transcribed the weather request correctly, then exposed central current-info classification and web-read tool-selection debt; focused route-contract tests now cover the central fix. A later practical flight lookup exposed a related prevention gap: executable-backed web tools could be advertised when enabled even if the daemon environment could not resolve their executable, despite durable path settings such as `web_tools.ghola_path`. Runtime model-visible tool registration now validates executable-backed tools before advertising them, and malformed or missing `web_search_url` disables `web_search` instead of falling back to a consumer search page. The `2026-05-18` Chat Connector photo proof then exposed webhook/polling media parity debt and generic-document image downgrade. Both were fixed centrally: webhook media is hydrated before pipeline ingress, and media ingress classifies `image/*` documents as canonical image evidence. The rerun used central vision preprocessing through embedded-runtime/gemma3:4b-it-qat and final answer route cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-pro. User-facing Chat Connector delivery was confirmed. The same run exposed verifier/recovery duplicate vision preprocessing; turn-scoped memoization is required so the same media evidence is not reinterpreted repeatedly inside one turn. A `2026-05-19` Chat Connector current-info correction drill completed on cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-pro with a long-running typing indicator and a bounded MeteoSwiss answer after operator correction from phonetic misrecognition to Meyrin. Exact-secret keystore CLI truth has regression coverage. The next rerun exposed a central follow-up policy and guard finalization defect: the correction turn lost web-read tools because the active live-info topic was not carried into turn-envelope policy, and the cloud provider pseudo tool-call markup containing `tool_call` leaked to Chat Connector after bounded retries. Focused central-pipeline regressions now cover policy-only prior live-info inheritance for corrections, raw tool_call protocol rejection outside fenced code, internal execution-state leakage rejection, and fail-closed framework wording. The targeted `fresh_external_info_correction_preserves_web_read` behavior soak passed against the isolated hydrated clone with `embedded-runtime/local-model-b`, score `100`/grade `A`, zero release-gate log findings, and zero stale retry-model waits. The latest Chat Connector voice/current-info rerun then exposed three central gaps: STT misheard Meyrin, Search Engine B returned a CAPTCHA challenge that was treated as search output, and Piper service startup ignored configured installed voice IDs when the packaged default model was absent. It also showed that the task-deferral guard could accept finalization after one failed selected web-read even though another selected web-read tool remained unused. Piper startup now resolves configured installed voices, CAPTCHA/search-provider challenge pages now return tool errors, `web_search` requires a configured JSON search API/gateway instead of Search Engine B/Provider C browser scraping, product-knowledge/wiki guidance now explains search credential setup, and task-deferral/live-web-evidence logic now requires successful web-read results or exhaustion of selected web-read alternates before finalization. The 2026-05-20 normal-surface Chat Connector rerun showed the cloud provider emitted two tool calls, but reflection then claimed no live-data or browsing capability because context construction preserved the latest user message while dropping the newer assistant/tool-result chunk under negative budget pressure. Context construction now preserves the latest user plus all newer current-turn evidence as a mandatory suffix, current-events truth guards reject first-person live-data capability denials, and live-evidence/tool-action turns now record structured required action evidence. The follow-up rerun showed provider-native required tool-choice is not universally supported by Provider A-compatible providers: the cloud provider rejected `tool_choice=required`, and an Embedded Runtime fallback produced plain-text `functionCall` JSON. Provider behavior now records required-tool-choice rejection and request marshalling degrades unsupported required tool choice to `auto`; text-form `functionCall` payloads are parsed and stripped at the LLM response boundary. Normal-surface current-info and realistic interaction evidence remains tracked by manual-agent-exercise and final release validation.

Focused the cloud provider proofs exposed central benchmark-validity issues: cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-pro answered TOOL_USE:C2 first with pseudo-tool XML, then with XML-like fabricated observation tags, then with a different tool-shaped XML tag. Required-tool benchmark rows now prove actual tool execution through phase telemetry, not answer text alone, and tool-shaped XML protocol leaks are rejected centrally. Full local rerun then exposed one correction_semantics verifier-repair wait where a single observed filename was followed by a relevance question and the model invented broader candidates; the central pre-model projection now finalizes evidence-limited single-candidate relevance follow-ups before inference. The 2026-05-24 motorcycle-route Chat Connector failure showed that an HTTP 200 status from a non-content endpoint can still look like successful web evidence if the soak only asks whether a web tool succeeded. required.title: Extensive manual agent exercise
required.summary: Before release, FlowForge must be manually exercised as a real agent across normal user surfaces, not only through scripted soaks. The Web text drill exposed a central continuation/tool-surface defect: after a the requested city-to-Dubai live-data planning turn, the short follow-up `give me the whole dataset` retained the natural-language objective but tool pruning ranked only the short latest utterance, leaving the focused web-read continuation with no selected web tools. The daemon tool-pruning query now includes current-turn execution context, and focused regressions prove the dataset continuation preserves the travel-planning objective, keeps the focused web-read surface, and ranks tools from that expanded objective. A 2026-05-26 WebSocket dashboard rerun then exposed the broader systemic variant: dataset/source/evidence continuations that follow a prior live-info request but do not pass through the short-followup expansion path could still drop the focused web-read surface. Central policy context and tool-pruning now inherit prior live-info objective context for dataset/source/evidence continuations. The same rerun also exposed an evidence-projection defect where generic web-read wrappers and transport headers (`Result`, `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials`) could replace useful model output. The web-read evidence summarizer now filters generic wrappers and transport headers before projecting evidence. Focused regressions and a rebuilt WebSocket dashboard proof show the continuation selects web-read tools and the first response remains operator-meaningful instead of transport metadata. A follow-on WebSocket manual drill exposed a central verifier-recovery regression: a correct current-session trip recall answer was rejected as only covering 1/2 requested parts because `Keep it to one sentence` was treated as a semantic subgoal; recovery then substituted stale older trip memory. Shared output-shape parsing and current-session continuity verification now cover that class, and the rebuilt daemon proof answered the same current-session recall with the requested city to Chamonix while stale Dubai memory was still present. The 2026-05-27 Web manual correction drill then exposed a central truth/recovery defect: a useful weather timestamp reconciliation was rejected because structured weather scalar values from tool JSON were not considered successful evidence use, and bounded recovery projected a static WorldWeatherOnline CDN icon URL as user-facing evidence. Truth checks now extract useful scalar web evidence from structured tool output, and recovery suppresses static asset URLs. The rerun delivered a normal bounded answer, with guard retry disabled and no CDN evidence projection. A later Web current-info turn exposed the same finalization family: the model had successful the requested city weather evidence but the user-visible answer claimed all live-data tools failed and zero current weather observations existed. The truth guard now retries blanket web-read failure claims that contradict successful current-turn web-read value evidence. The same manual Web pass exposed dashboard-local presentation-state loss: topic refreshes could wipe unsent active-session text and interrupt the thinking cursor during an in-flight send. The dashboard now persists active-session drafts by session ID until successful send, avoids session re-render over active send/thinking state, and labels final-only in-flight sends as thinking rather than streaming. The drill remains in progress for broader normal-surface manual coverage. The 2026-05-29 autonomous manual-agent-exercise pass (driven against the isolated dev daemon before handing the nose-for-tender-spots probing to the operator) found the central behavioral-guard grounding (commits a6ba976d/51e55835) holds on a capable model: false-completion on a hypothetical, legitimate task-deferral, grounded financial refusal+explanation, current-time/current-price, and continuation turns (acknowledgement + honest non-completion) all responded cleanly on cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-pro. The local-model-b current-info fail-closes observed during probing were model weakness, not guard over-fires — the same prompts do not fail-close on a capable model, matching the slice-4 environmental classification — so no guard fix was warranted there. The pass did surface two real user-facing failure-presentation defects, now fixed (commit 530cfbcd): (1) non-panic failures delivered canned jargon strings ('model failed before producing a verified answer', 'turn could not be completed') that are gobbledygook to users; (2) the live-web-evidence recovery templated transport-only HTTP headers (Cf-Cache-Status, then Alternate-Protocol) into a fabricated 'Source evidence' answer. Fixes: framework fallbacks now speak in plain human language as the platform ('we', never the agent's first-person 'I') with an honest attempt count ('After N attempts, we were unable to get a meaningful response from the model.'), and transport-metadata classification now recognizes HTTP response-header grammar generically (a single hyphenated token key) instead of an incomplete allow-list, so no raw header is ever templated as source content. New project rules canned-responses-panic-only and the we/I voice delineation are recorded in docs/architext/data/rules.json. Separately, the release fuzz gate surfaced an O(n^2) algorithmic-complexity sharp edge in the L1 prompt-injection decoder (decodeHTMLEntities scanned to end-of-string for an entity terminator); fixed by bounding the search to the window the code already requires (commit 08efb1ce, bounded in production by the existing 100KB input cap). All fixes live-verified against the dev daemon: a weak model now yields the plain-voice attempt-count message and header-only web evidence no longer produces a fabricated answer. The source-URL mis-attribution is now fixed (commit d28b6ba4): the live-evidence recovery answered an ETH-price turn as 'Source evidence from `https://reference.example.com {...}' because parseWebReadEvidence body-scraped the first URL in the tool output and found one inside a Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header value; the actual fetched URL (api.coingecko.com) lives in the tool input, not the output. The body-scrape fallback was removed so only an explicit URL:/Page URL:/Source URL: line is attributed, never a URL scraped from a body or header value; live-verified — the same turn now correctly attributes the CoinGecko source. One cosmetic-only sliver remains: when the observed value is a JSON API body it is surfaced verbatim (e.g. {"Region East":{"usd":2001.86}}) rather than as a clean human number. Rendering it cleanly requires the guard to accept the model's own reflected value so the raw-evidence recovery is not needed; that is the scheduled central guard-grounding-judge follow-up (a fragile token-overlap heuristic would violate systemic-over-ad-hoc), not a standalone spot fix. A continued 2026-05-30 manual-exercise pass fixed two more user-facing-honesty defects, both TDD'd (and the leak live-verified against ~/.flowforge-dev (a) ConfiguredAccessShortcut and IntrospectionShortcut returned runtime-owned grounding snapshots verbatim as the user answer, leaking model-facing scaffolding (authoritative-state headers plus the 'Do not infer access from installed skills...' model-directed instruction) — the shortcuts were removed, the configured-access snapshot is now injected into the model prompt under a '## Configured Access' section so the model answers in platform voice, and SystemPromptLeakGuard was extended with the scaffolding signatures as defense-in-depth (commit e2ed363f; Architext decision capability-access-grounded-not-shortcut); live-verified — a calendar/email-access turn now returns a clean grounded answer with no scaffolding, react_turns=2 on cloud-provider-v4-pro. (b) Context-window overflow failures surfaced the generic 'unable to get a meaningful response from the model' message instead of attributing the cause; a core.ErrContextCapacity sentinel is now classified at the provider error-parse seam and attributed by UserVisibleFailureMessage, ranked above the generic ErrLLM case (commit d66e30f3). The 2026-05-30 pass also delivered the tool-defs introspection refactor — the systemic root-cause fix for production issue #1 (context-budget exhaustion squeezing out memory): the per-turn tool surface is now a small always-native floor plus an embedder-ranked catalog hydrated on demand via a request_tools meta-tool, shrinking the resident schema bulk (live-verified native selected_tool_count 6, down from ~15) and removing the recall ceiling. Built via a 10-task TDD plan (commits a860655..8dec5016), all packages green, Architext decision tool-surface-catalog-hydration. 2026-06-10 interactive exercise (operator-directed, cloud-provider-v4-flash, isolated dev clone): 12 edge probes across artifact-write, inspection continuation, sarcasm/correction, reply-only contracts, capability honesty, and pending-action displacement. Result — honesty intact (no fabrication; A1/A2 executed real write+readback; declines were truthful), and the sweep's guard fixes confirmed live (sincere-praise produced no false apology; reply-only returned the literal). One weak-model dead-end found and fixed: cloud-provider reached for an off-surface `bash` to list a directory and gave up instead of pivoting to the pinned `list_directory`; the out-of-surface tool rejection now enumerates the available tools so the model recovers (loop.go), live-verified to pivot and return concrete inspection results.
required.rationale: Behavior soaks are necessary but insufficient. The release must prove that normal agent surfaces behave coherently under realistic operator interaction, including ambiguity, corrections, multimodal turns, current information, tool availability, failure handling, and continuation turns where the latest user utterance is too short to independently select the required tool surface.

Live email exercise over-fire dropped from react_turns=4 to react_turns=1. Eval harness (build tag eval) enforces safety-critical 'never forgive a genuine violation' metric. Architext decision central-grounding-judge.

grounding judge IN-STACK validation + observability fix (2026-05-31): re-running the judge through the real daemon (production config/router/full llm.Service on cloud-provider-v4-pro, not a bare harness) revealed the judge's decision was UNOBSERVABLE on the decline/error path — only forgiveness emitted an event, the forgive closure logged nothing, and Adjudicate ran on context.Background() so it was absent from the turn trace. A silent 6s timeout was indistinguishable from a correct uphold (Rule 14 / no-silent-failures gap). Fixed (TDD): GroundingVerdict gains an Outcome (forgave/upheld/error) and the forgive closure now emits a grounding_judge_consulted timed event (outcome + reason + latency + guard) for EVERY consultation. In-stack live validation on ~/.flowforge-dev then OBSERVED the judge: forgave task_deferral + subagent_claim over-fires (honest 'checking sub-agent infra'/'no sub-agent tooling' answers), UPHELD a genuine false-delegation claim, was not consulted for non-forgivable guards (internal_jargon), all at ~2.0-2.8s (well under the 6s budget — feature is live, not inert). Local eval harness (local-model-a) scored 4/4 over-fire recall + 3/3 safety upholds. context.Background() left as-is: telemetry achieves observability without coupling the adjudication to turn cancellation. Eval harness made runnable (env-driven model/key/timeout; corrected the double-/v1 base-URL instruction that 404'd).

grounding judge forgivable-set tightened (2026-05-31): on review, a falsely claimed completion or delegation is trust-critical (the user asked for something to be carried out; claiming it was done when it was not destroys trust and leaves the task undone), so execution_truth and subagent_claim were removed from forgivableGuards — the judge can no longer soften them, matching the existing financial_action_truth hard policy. task_deferral stays forgivable, so the original capability-denial over-fire ('I can't read your email, it isn't configured') is still rescued. Accepted cost: honest answers that merely use completion/delegation phrasing exhaust the retry budget before standing. TDD (TestGuardIsForgivable moves both guards to the must-never-forgive set); Architext central-grounding-judge decision updated; eval dataset re-aligned (over-fire case re-keyed to task_deferral; the execution_truth/subagent_claim safety cases retained as defense-in-depth on Adjudicate's raw judgment).

manual-agent-exercise finding + fix — bare MCP tool names (2026-05-31): operator weather/flight session (session 8faa17de) surfaced Duncan unable to navigate MeteoSwiss because request_tools for browser_navigate/snapshot/tabs was 'rejected / not in the catalog', forcing it to ask the operator to adjust the tool surface. Root cause: MCP tools register namespaced (mcp__playwright__browser_navigate) but the model references the bare suffix (browser_navigate); request_tools hydration used exact reg.Get only, and the surface gate's alias resolver was a curated map that did not cover arbitrary MCP tools. Fixed (commit bb59ebda, TDD): shared exact->unique-suffix resolver wired into Registry.ResolveName (hydration) and CanonicalToolNameForSelectedSurface (surface gate); ambiguous cross-server bare names resolve to nothing (never guess). Live-verified on ~/.flowforge-dev a bare browser_navigate call resolved to mcp__playwright__browser_navigate and executed (tool_call_finished=success), the prior rejection gone. SECONDARY OBSERVATION (not yet fixed): in one probe request_tools itself was tool_not_selected for the turn (the model noted 'request_tools isn't available this turn'); the tool-surface-catalog-hydration decision requires request_tools to stay in the always-native floor, so floor membership under query-ranked surfaces is worth a follow-up audit.

request_tools floor-membership audit + fix (2026-05-31, commit e08e39db): the secondary observation above was root-caused to ToolProfileFocusedWebRead. filterToolDefsForFocusedWebRead kept only OperationWebRead tools and dropped everything else including request_tools, violating the always-native-floor invariant. On a web-read turn this stranded interactive navigation: the model could call browser_navigate (web-read) but neither request_tools ('not available this turn') nor browser_click (OperationExecution, also dropped) — the operator's 'I can see where to click but the click tool keeps getting blocked' MeteoSwiss symptom. Fixed (TDD): the focused filter now keeps web-read tools PLUS the request_tools hydration bootstrap, while still dropping memory/runtime-context (the FocusedWebReadExcludesRuntimeContext invariant is preserved). Live-verified: the previously failing 'Call request_tools ... navigate ...' probe now runs with zero tool_not_selected events and fetches the page. REMAINING follow-up (not yet fixed): OperationClassForName classifies bare names, so namespaced MCP web tools (mcp__playwright__browser_navigate) miss OperationWebRead and are reachable in focused turns only via request_tools hydration — a classification gap worth closing so namespaced MCP web tools are natively available.

MCP tool namespacing (2026-05-31, commit 15c6fb78): closing the above follow-up surfaced that the deeper issue was the OPPOSITE of the stale mcp_bridge comment — MCP tools were registered under RAW bare names (not namespaced), so cross-server tool-name collisions were possible (SyncMCPTools had skip-on-collision logic). Decision mcp-tool-namespacing: RegisterMCPTools/SyncMCPTools now register MCP tools as mcp__server__tool (MCPToolName), the bridge keeps the raw name (serverToolName()) for the actual mcp.CallTool, and bare-name resolution (Registry.ResolveName, CanonicalToolNameForSelectedSurface) + namespace-stripping classification (OperationClassForName/bareMCPToolName) make the model's bare-name usage resolve and classify correctly. This makes the earlier resolution/classification commits (bb59ebda + the OperationClassForName change) load-bearing rather than speculative. TDD across MCPToolName round-trip, serverToolName raw-name selection, OperationClassForName namespace-strip, ResolveName/CanonicalToolNameForSelectedSurface suffix. HONEST live-verification gap: namespaced names (mcp__playwright__browser_navigate) are offered with zero tool_not_selected, but cloud-provider preferred http_fetch in every probe so the namespaced playwright Execute path was not model-exercised live this session; serverToolName() unit-proves the raw-name server call. Earlier-this-session correction: the original 'request_tools rejected' blocker was the FocusedWebRead floor (e08e39db), not name resolution — bb59ebda was a no-op until namespacing made it load-bearing; recorded transparently rather than presented as the root-cause fix.

MCP Execute gap closed + steering attribution corrected (2026-05-31, commit b6c59104): the operator challenged the claim that 'cloud-provider preferred http_fetch'. Correct — it was OUR steering, not the model's preference: the web-read turn prompt (pipeline_run_stages.go:1107) explicitly says 'prefer direct source/API fetches before generic browser search', and MCPLatencyPenalty=0.05 (tool_search.go) down-ranks every MCP tool below the non-MCP http_fetch in surface selection. Because that steering is exactly what blocked a live MCP execution, the namespaced Execute path was closed DETERMINISTICALLY instead: a narrow mcpToolCaller seam + fake-caller test (TestMcpBridgeTool_ExecuteCallsServerWithRawName) proves Execute dispatches the RAW tool name (browser_navigate) to CallTool even though the registry/model name is namespaced — model-independent. RELATED OBSERVATION (not changed): the same steering (web-read prompt + MCP penalty) systematically deprioritizes MCP browser tools, so interactive web tasks that genuinely need browser_click/type may be steered to http_fetch (which can't interact) — worth revisiting whether interactive-web turns should relax the 'prefer fetch' instruction / MCP penalty.

MCP class penalty removed — tools ranked on relevance only (2026-06-01, commit b64e525f): operator principle — 'why should MCP have different scoring than anything else?' RankTools subtracted a flat MCPLatencyPenalty (0.05) from every MCP tool's cosine score, a per-source thumb on the scale (not a relevance signal) that quietly down-ranked MCP tools below builtins (browser_* below http_fetch) regardless of fit. Removed the penalty entirely — the agenttools + core.Config struct fields, the mcp_latency_penalty TOML knob, the default, the daemon plumbing, and the RankTools application — so an MCP tool and a builtin with the same embedding score identically. Latency/cost is a separate axis; if it ever warrants ranking influence it must be a measured, uniform signal across ALL tools, not a flat per-source penalty. TDD: TestRankTools_ScoresMCPLikeAnyOtherTool. This removes one of the two MCP-deprioritization forces from the prior observation; the web-read 'prefer fetch over browser' prompt remains (separate, intentional for data-fetch turns).

Sub-agent delegation thrash + raw web-read error leak fixed (2026-06-01, commits 42539700 + d7edbc18). A 'delegate a research task to a sub-agent to compile a report' probe sent the model into a 13-turn thrash: the turn-policy classifier matched 'compile a ... report' and routed the turn to a web-read/authoring envelope whose per-turn filter strips every orchestration tool, so the registered compose-subagent/orchestrate-subagents tools were filtered off the surface each iteration and request_tools (the catalog bootstrap) was rejected too — the model could see the orchestration tools in the catalog but never load them (closing the evidence[41] open observation). Fix (TDD): looksLikeSubagentDelegationTask routes explicit delegation to a new ToolProfileFocusedDelegation that admits OperationDelegation + capability-inventory + task-inspection + runtime classes (mirroring FocusedScheduling), and request_tools is kept by name in filterToolDefsForPolicy for every focused profile (universal catalog-bootstrap invariant, mirroring the focused-web-read special-case). Architext decision subagent-delegation-tool-profile. Live-verified on ~/.flowforge-dev 13->4 react turns, sub-agent roster reachable, request_tools successfully hydrates orchestrate-subagents ('Loaded: orchestrate-subagents. Call them directly now.'), clean honest answer the grounding judge forgives. Separately, the 'We couldn't reach the sources needed to answer this: ...' blocker leaked the raw first line of a failed web-read tool's output verbatim (observed: the SSRF guard's 'fetching from private or reserved addresses is not allowed'); safeSourceReadFailureReason now humanizes security-guard internals, omits raw implementation detail (error JSON, stack traces, panics) so the caller falls back to the generic blocker, and preserves concrete request-level failures (HTTP status, provider challenge) per the existing TestBlockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion contract; raw detail stays in the turn trace. Open follow-up (not a surface defect): the model does not always proactively chain request_tools -> orchestrate-subagents on a natural delegation turn — a prompt-steering nuance akin to the MCP browser-vs-fetch observation.

cross-turn nav looping + topic-shift over-persistence remediation (2026-06-02, commits 2ee7b34e + 1cf0945d; plan docs/plans/2026-06-02-cross-turn-looping-topic-shift-remediation.md). The Chat Connector MeteoSwiss/Dubai exercise (session 8faa17de) showed Duncan re-attempting a dead URL (meteoswiss .../meyrin.html, a 404) turn after turn and failing to pivot when the operator changed topic. ROOT CAUSE confirmed against code: the ReAct loop is rebuilt per turn (its in-turn failedCalls dedup cannot see cross-turn repeats) AND loadSession never rehydrates 'tool' messages (the model loses all memory of failed navigations). Defect A fix (2ee7b34e, TDD): a 404 is recorded by the fetch tool as a SUCCESSFUL result whose body carries the HTTP status line, so failures are classified from the output not the status column (agenttools.IsPermanentWebFailure — permanent = 404/403/410/DNS/SSRF; transient = timeout/5xx/429 and unclassifiable are never blocked, so a recoverable source is never suppressed). pipeline.restoreRecentFailedSources (mirrors restoreRecentInspectionRoot) seeds the session with normalized-URL failure counts (agenttools.WebSourceFingerprint collapses host-case/trailing-slash/fragment variants); the loop folds the cross-turn count into its existing failCount>=2 suppression; and a '## Previously Failed Sources' prompt hint tells the model not to re-attempt the dead source so it can pivot. Defect B framing was sharpened during implementation: substantive (long) topic-shift messages already pass through ContextualizeShortFollowup unchanged — there is no code path that steers them to the pending action, so the observed 'didn't pivot' was Defect A, not B. The genuine B path is a SHORT topic-divergent message lexically misclassified as a continuation; fix (1cf0945d, TDD) adds an embedder margin-based gate (GuardSemanticClassifier.PendingActionTopicDiverges) that protects generic confirmations ('ok'/'please do') via an affirmation centroid and only displaces the stale pending action on a confident topic shift, failing safe to resume on any error. Affected suites green non-race and -race (agent/pipeline/session/daemon). OPEN: the Defect B real-embedder soak (TestPendingActionTopicDivergenceSoak, build tag soak) could not run in this environment (local embedded-runtime 0.30.0 missing llama-server binary, all embeds fail); thresholds are reasoned defaults (= CompletionAssertionThreshold) pending a soak run on a healthy embedder host, and the live MeteoSwiss re-run on ~/.flowforge-dev remains for the operator manual-exercise.
internal/agent/loop.go (out-of-surface tool rejection enumerates available tools; manual-exercise steering fix, live-verified pivot)

The same audit exposed misleading cloud-cost labels and high-volume INFO noise for zero-work curation loops.

required.title: Tool-call empty-response shaping, per-model ReAct timeout, and 2b grounding decision
required.summary: Two committed behavioral fixes plus a data-backed architecture decision from a cross-session memory-grounding A/B soak. (1) Tool-call turns no longer surface as empty responses: the ReAct loop synthesizes terminal content from tool results on every non-error exit (the wall-clock-deadline path previously did not, unlike StateDone), and a genuinely empty result is reported as a failure (ErrLLM) so guard_retry propagates it once instead of the empty_response guard silently retrying to exhaustion (commit 153b9b18). (2) The ReAct loop deadline now scales with the model's per-call timeout (new Service.ModelCallTimeout x shared TurnBudgetForCallTimeout, 3x capped 15m) so slow local models such as embedded-runtime/local-model-a (~200s/call) are not killed mid-call by the model-blind 90s AutonomyMaxTurnDurationSecs default; the floor only raises the ceiling, so fast turns are unaffected (commit 8af9e17f). (3) A new cross-session grounding instrument (scripts/run-memory-grounding-soak.py) ran a clean Arm A vs Arm B comparison on local-model-a: with the retrieved block injected (HEAD) 3/4 comparable facts recalled with 0 confabulation; the 2b index-only variant collapsed to 0/4 with confabulation rising. ROOT CAUSE (corrected 2026-06-05 from the tool_calls DB; an earlier claim that the model never invokes recall came from a broken metric that read only the final API response and missed in-loop ReAct tool calls): the model DOES call recall_memory/search_memories. Index-only fails because it stacks three failure points the injected block avoids - (a) empty/think-only turns that never call a tool, (b) recall_memory(id) misuse without a search_memories(query) fallback, (c) extraction failure from a successful search. The fallback works when the model searches and extracts cleanly (launch_date passed via search_memories). DECISION: keep the injected retrieved block; do NOT make memory tiers index-only (2b rejected for now). Part 1+2a already delivered the context-budget/efficiency win and fixed the home=Berlin confabulation. Revisit 2b only after those three failure points are closed (reliable model output, search_memories steering, better extraction). The instrument's recall_tool_used metric has been fixed to read in-loop tool calls from the cloned DB.
required.rationale: Surfaced during the cross-session grounding soak: tool-using recall turns silently looped to exhaustion via the content-only empty_response guard, and slow-model turns were killed mid-call by the global 90s loop deadline, which together confounded the 2b measurement. Both are fixed and unit-tested (TDD). The clean A/B then showed the injected block is materially more robust than index-only on local-model-a DB ground truth (tool_calls) corrected the root cause: the model does invoke recall_memory/search_memories; index-only fails on empty-think turns, recall_memory(id) misuse, and extraction failures rather than on a never-called fallback.

required.title: Memory recall collapsed into one polymorphic, tier-blind verb
required.summary: Collapsed recall_memory + search_memories into one polymorphic recall_memory(memory_id?, query?): an id resolves to full de-tiered content, an id-miss with a query falls back to topic search, and a bare topic returns a ranked legible preview list (each row carries a recall id for pull-by-id). This closes two of the three index-only failure points named in memory-grounding-loop-robustness - FP2 (recall_memory(id) dead-end with no search fallback) and FP3 (json.MarshalIndent value-burying): the model can no longer strand on a bad id, and topic results render value-shaped lines instead of a JSON dump. Empty-args calls backfill the query from the last user message, giving unconscious topic recall. Memory tiering is now fully abstracted from agent awareness - source_table is removed from the tool schema, tool output, and the injected memory index; tiers are retained internally (promotion/demotion, scoring, housekeeping) but invisible to the transaction. Facts for the verifier/index harvester travel via a machine-only Result.Metadata envelope (memory_facts), decoupled from the de-tiered model-facing output (recall facts now carry a flat 0.75 confidence since the envelope does not carry per-row confidence). search_memories is fully retired across all internal sites: the MemorySearchTool type and its two registrations, the recall floor, native/default AlwaysInclude lists, memory-read semantics, the focused-memory daemon adapter, the truthfulness guard + proactive-action synthesizer, the system prompt + pipeline fallback text, the injected index footer, the fact harvester, and the eval-exercise harness. Built via an 11-task TDD plan; full suite green (58 packages, 0 fail/0 vet). FP1 (think-only zero-tool turns) and 2b (index-only tiers) remain explicitly out of scope.
required.rationale: A failed recall_memory(id) had no query to fall back on (an id is not a topic), so the tool must accept a topic and let code do the routing rather than steering the model between two tools (Rule 6: if code can answer, code answers). Tiering is an internal organization/persistence/efficiency concern; a human does not consciously 'retrieve from short-term memory', so the agent's relationship to memory is purely transactional and tier-blind. Fully retiring the name now (rather than keeping a deprecating alias) is correct because every consumer is internal and updatable atomically - a delegating alias kept 'for later' is exactly the band-aid/TODO this repo forbids.
evidence: the unit-test suite ./... (58 packages green, 0 fail)

required.title: Embedder embed-model task-prefix foundation + runtime pin/assert + recalibration
required.summary: Fixes the embedder-soak regression recorded under final-release-gates. RCA proved it environmental: an embedded-runtime auto-update (0.24.0->0.30.6) shifted embed-model output past the calibrated pending-action divergence floor (code + GGUF byte-identical 422e2400..HEAD). Root fix (10-task TDD): (1) embed embed-model with its required task-instruction prefixes via a typed EmbedTask at the single embed seam (search_query/search_document/classification/clustering; the divergence guard shares the classifier centroids so it uses the classification task); (2) pin the embedder runtime (memory.expected_embedder_runtime=0.30.6) and make models embedder-check + startup probe /api/version and fail loud on drift; (3) version-tag stored vectors (embedding_version = model|prefix-scheme|runtime) so a model/prefix/runtime change re-embeds them in place via the consolidation backfill instead of mixing incompatible vectors; (4) recalibrate the divergence floor from the observed cluster gap under the pinned, prefixed embedder (broadened bare-affirmation anchors; floor 0.0, margin 0.05). Result: all 4 embedder soaks PASS (0 false-positives, 3/4 shifts caught), the other guards' thresholds held under the prefixed embeddings (no recalibration needed). Floors remain absolute; relative-decision redesign and runtime bundling deferred.
required.rationale: We do not ship with a regression. The embedder is a foundational invariant for behavioral correctness; an unpinned runtime made calibrated floors silently fragile. Prefixing puts embed-model in-distribution (larger, steadier margins); pinning+asserting turns drift into a loud gate failure; versioned re-embed keeps persisted vectors consistent. This is the systemic root fix, not a floor nudge (nudging would have traded one false-positive for a false-negative on an explicit topic switch).
evidence: scripts/run-embedder-soak.sh: EMBEDDER SOAK PASS (all 4) on HEAD post-fix

required.title: Adversarial regression harness (first slice)
required.summary: Systemic adversarial regression harness: a declarative issue registry (testdata/adversarial/issues/*.json) where each exposed issue links to a Release Truth item and carries hand-authored adversarial variants (paraphrase / context-pressure / continuation) + a deterministic failure signature. A deterministic check library (scripts/adversarial_checks.py), a registry loader with load-time validation (scripts/adversarial_registry.py), a live runner reusing the behavior-soak clone driver against ~/.flowforge-dev on :8080 (scripts/run-adversarial-soak.py; clone-mode + local-model rails, per-variant error isolation, exit non-zero only on a release_gate regression), an in-process the platform unit runner data-driving the divergence guard against the real pinned embedder (src/engine/adversarial_soak_test.ts, build tag soak), and a coverage report mapping cases to the bug-fix corpus (scripts/adversarial_coverage.py). Seeded with 6 cases (memory single-verb, memory confabulation, web-evidence transport leak, source-URL mis-attribution, canned-response voice, pending-action divergence). Built subagent-driven with per-task spec+quality review (reviews caught a header-regex false-positive, a must_cite_tool _tool_calls key bug, and a Critical per-variant error-isolation gap, all fixed). Verified: all Python unit tests pass; the platform unit soak passes (3 divergence checks, continuation never displaced); the live runner was observed running real multi-turn clone-agent conversations end-to-end (session threading + checks applying) for ~43 min before the slow local-model sweep was terminated externally mid-request (no internal error/traceback, well under the 1800s per-request timeout; cause not definitively determined — a task/UI stop or OS memory pressure under local-model-a ~9GB; OOM-vs-manual not verified). The full live-tier verdict across all cases was therefore NOT captured; it remains an operator-run (see the planned follow-on). Coverage report: 3/36 bug-fixes covered, 33 gaps surfaced loudly.
required.rationale: Previously-exposed issues were guarded by fragmented soaks with no single source of truth that each issue stays dead, and the soaks probed happy paths rather than the guard edges where regressions reappear. The registry makes coverage auditable and gaps loud; the deterministic unit tier is gating-ready now, while the full live-tier sweep is an operator-run (slow under a local model, like manual-agent-exercise).
evidence: the unit-test suite -tags soak -run TestAdversarialUnitSoak ./src/engine/ (PASS, 3 checks); scripts pytest (checks/registry/coverage) all PASS

required.title: Adversarial harness: behavioral backfill + honest coverage denominator
required.summary: Second slice of the adversarial harness. Added 3 genuine behavioral cases (ADV-TOOL-CALL-LEAK and ADV-MEM-TOOL-NOISE — live, via a new must_not_leak_tool_call deterministic check that matches structural tool-call leak markers, not prose; ADV-TOPIC-SWITCH — unit, via a new inverse the platform check must_displace_pending_action proving a confident cross-domain switch IS detected, which also closes a false-green hole where a degenerate always-continue guard would have passed every must-not-displace case). Made the coverage denominator honest: testdata/adversarial/out_of_scope.json classifies the 21 raw bug-fixes that are NOT black-box agent-behavioral (install/CI/upgrade, macOS daemon lifecycle, evidence CLI, wire serialization, internal retrieval ranking, Windows repair messaging, soak-coverage meta) WITH a per-item justification; adversarial_coverage.py now reports behavioral coverage (40.0%, 6/15) alongside the raw figure (16.7%) and fails loud on any stale out-of-scope id. No fake cases were added to inflate the metric. The ADV-TOPIC-SWITCH probe also surfaced a documented guard boundary: the topic-similarity divergence guard does not displace on SAME-domain explicit-cancel redirects ('forget the flight, book a hotel instead') — a deliberate safe false-negative (over-persistence, never auto-execute). That aspirational behavior (explicit-cancellation speech-act detection) was routed to the roadmap with justification rather than changing a freshly-recalibrated safety-critical guard mid-release. 9 behavioral gaps remain open and visible (5 streaming no-silent-failure cases needing provider fault injection, artifact-write-proof needing an FS-aware check, capability-denial + provider-metadata-truth needing the model-judge tier, full-memory-dump context-bounding) — all aligned with the planned discovery/live-gate follow-on.
required.rationale: Backfill is incremental and the coverage report keeps gaps loud. The remaining 9 behavioral gaps each need a new check capability (fault injection, FS-aware assertion, or the model-judge tier), so they are sequenced into the discovery/live-gate follow-on rather than forced now. Classifying non-behavioral corpus items out of scope (each justified) makes the headline metric meaningful instead of permanently capped by infra fixes that have no chat-observable signature.
evidence: the unit-test suite -tags soak -run TestAdversarialUnitSoak ./src/engine/ (PASS, 6 checks); scripts pytest 22 passed; coverage 40.0% behavioral / 16.7% raw, 0 stale

required.title: DB maintenance consolidated into self-healing --repair, routed through the daemon
required.summary: Productionizes the FTS-corruption-resilience investigation. Root cause of the recurring production 'memory_fts corruption' was identified as a transient false positive: full-text-search quick_check run against the LIVE DB races concurrent trigger writes + WAL checkpoints (HEAD's maintenance.go already labels it 'transient live FTS/WAL check'; durability PRAGMAs are sound — WAL + synchronous=NORMAL + mmap_size=0). The v1.0.12 production binary lacks that snapshot-disambiguation, which is why it surfaced needs_manual_action + blocked backups. Shipped: Store.RunMaintenance centralizes the maintenance pipeline (snapshot-safe integrity, FTS rebuild, prune, vacuum); self-healing --repair is the single entry point and routes the heavy maintenance through the running daemon over the WS control seam (db.maintenance.run, online-safe incremental vacuum) instead of opening the live DB from a second process (a cross-process corruption vector), falling back to a local full VACUUM when no daemon is up; daemon liveness uses a control-seam Ping, not an internal REST call (architecture guard green). The defrag subcommand is retired — DB maintenance folded into self-healing --repair, code-quality scanner rehomed to `go run ./scripts/codescan`. Verified: internal/db, internal/api, internal/daemoncontrol, cmd, cmd/admin tests green; gofmt/vet/build/the linter(0) clean; TestArchitecture_ProductSurfacesDoNotUseInternalREST passes.
required.rationale: The user runs the v1.0.12 binary, where the missing snapshot-disambiguation surfaces transient fts checks as corruption and blocks backups; upgrading off v1.0.12 already neutralizes the symptom. This change additionally removes the second-process maintenance corruption vector and unifies the maintenance surface. Runtime-prevention hardening (auto-rebuild-from-source, snapshot-only heartbeat, single-writer pool, routing the lightweight hygiene helpers) is deferred with justification per the decision record — defense-in-depth carrying hot-path risk, sequenced after the v1.1.0 cut.

required.title: Conversational response-cache removal (advisory-posture slice)
required.summary: The recurring turn-collapse ('Yes, please do' -> 'What's on your mind?') was a content-keyed response cache replaying a stale, context-free greeting for a short affirmation whose correct answer depended on the prior turn's pending action, not its words. Two cache tiers shared the semantic_cache table and both had to be gated: the pipeline semantic response cache (src/engine/pipeline_cache.go) and the lower, more aggressive n-gram-semantic llm cache (internal/llm/cache.go), whose only prior gate was len(req.Tools)>0. The conversational-turn predicate is now centralized in core.IsContextDependentPrompt (acknowledgements/affirmations/continuations and <=3-word follow-ups), and BOTH tiers bypass it for lookup AND store (pipeline_cache lookup+store; llm cache Get+Put). The pipeline wrapper keeps its embedding-exemplar augmentation on top of the shared predicate. The conversational-cache removal from the advisory-posture decision is now complete across both tiers; neither can resurrect the collapse, including via fuzzy n-gram match. Implementation is verified green (go build ./..., full the unit-test suite ./...; new core + llm bypass unit tests; the turn-collapse instrument is must_not_revert_to_greeting + the ADV-TURN-COLLAPSE adversarial case). GATE SATISFIED 2026-06-10: when this gate was written the only verification was unit-level; the adversarial live tier built since IS that operator probe, made permanent and repeatable. ADV-TURN-COLLAPSE reproduces the exact scenario on the hydrated normal surface — a setup turn establishing a real pending action ('My scheduled report didn't run this morning. Can you look into why?') followed by the literal collapse trigger ('Yes, please do', plus 'yes please' / 'go ahead' paraphrases) — running clone mode, which boots the REAL daemon via go run . serve with the cloned hydrated config and probes through the real /api/agent/message surface (real config resolution, not unit construction). Canonical + both paraphrases passed must_not_revert_to_greeting in three independent runs on 2026-06-10, including the final RC evidence run on frozen HEAD eae78ee5 (adversarial 27/27, cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-flash). The broader advisory-posture program (classify guard touchpoints safety-critical vs behavioral, lighten/remove behavioral ones and pre-model conversational shortcuts) stays gated on harness numbers per the decision and is next-cycle scope.
required.rationale: Behavioral correctness is paramount and outranks the remaining feature/collateral gates; the turn-collapse is a reproduced live harm and a release blocker. Removing only the pipeline cache left the collapse reachable through the lower llm cache's n-gram semantic match, so the removal had to extend to both tiers behind one shared predicate to be systemic rather than a spot fix. Earning each removal with a harness number (the turn-collapse instrument) preserves the pipeline while flipping its posture from overriding to advisory.

required.title: Live cron-flow verification: guard over-fire + intent misclassification fixes
required.summary: Operator dev-instance verification of the cron flow (run from source against ~/.flowforge-dev reproduced three behavioral defects, all three now fixed and live-verified. (1) execution_truth guard over-fired: the central COMPLETION_ASSERTION claim-vs-report signal scored honest third-person/historical cron-status reporting ('the daily_security_briefing last ran successfully on 2026-04-03') as a first-person false completion, rejecting Duncan's correct answers 4x and forcing retries/escalation. Fixed by adding third-person/historical reporting exemplars to nonCompletionExemplars (domain-agnostic, the architecture's stated fix path, not a per-category heuristic) + held-out cron-status cases in the guard embedder soak corpus. (2) 'please rerun the daily security briefing now' was misclassified as a lightweight conversational turn and routed to social Conversation Mode with zero tools -> raw tool_call leak -> internal_protocol rejection -> canned 'unusable internal output'. Root cause: classifyIntent lacked run/execute verbs so the 44-char imperative fell through the <50-char conversational default. Fixed by adding run/rerun/restart/execute/trigger/start/launch/invoke/kick-off to taskMarkers + a deterministic regression test. Live re-probe in a fresh session on the rebuilt daemon: 0 execution_truth rejections (was 4), both status questions answered honestly and on-topic; the rerun engages as an action and asks for the briefing definition (0 Conversation Mode misroutes, 0 protocol-leak/unusable-output). REMAINING (#3 of the cluster, tracked separately): executive-state fixation/bleed — a session-scoped executive task fixated on consolidating 'redundant' cron jobs (citing phantom jobs that don't exist) dominated the prompt's Working State and pulled unrelated answers toward redundancy. Deferred to its own deeper change (executive-state injection/decay/relevance gating); the guard over-fire was its amplifier and is now removed. UPDATE: #3 of the cluster (the executive/'weird-behavior' fixation) is also FIXED. Root cause traced via dev-instance DB forensics to STALE/FALSE DURABLE MEMORY, not executive state or cache: tool outputs were persisted as durable recallable memories (manager.go ingests every non-derivable tool message as an importance-7 tool_event), so retrieval resurfaced a stale one-time mutation ('cron: Created cron job run-health-check-and') as a current fact; the model trusted it over the live cron list and confabulated health-check jobs that no longer exist (only 1 exists), perseverating on consolidating 'redundant' jobs. Same mechanism as the self-funding/financial fixation. Three-axis fix: (axis2 formation hygiene) shouldSkipVolatileToolEventMemory blocks failures/rejections/empty-results/HTTP-transport-noise/state-mutations from durable memory; (axis1 recall grounding) the [Active Memory] block carries a precedence caveat that live tool/current-turn evidence supersedes recalled context for current/changeable state; (axis3 clean) one-time quarantine of 29 volatile tool-event rows incl. the phantom-job seed in ~/.flowforge-dev Live re-probe (fresh session, rebuilt daemon): 'are any cron jobs redundant or duplicated?' returns the actual 4 jobs, 0 phantom jobs, 0 redundancy confabulation, 0 execution_truth rejections. DONE (axis 3 systematized): repairVolatileToolEventMemories added to RunHygiene so `self-healing --repair` (and the daemon endpoint sharing RunHygiene) quarantines+stales pre-existing volatile tool-event rows on any instance incl. production, never manual SQL. Live-verified: seeded a volatile cron-mutation row in ~/.flowforge-dev, ran self-healing --repair -> volatile_tool_event_memories=repaired (quarantined 1, staled 1), row now memory_state='stale'.
required.rationale: Behavioral correctness is paramount and these are reproduced live harms on a core flow (cron status + rerun). Both fixes target the 'deterministic logic competes with the model' class central to the advisory-posture program: a behavioral guard was overriding honest model output, and an intent classifier was misrouting an action request to a zero-tool social path. Each fix is earned by a number (live re-probe deltas) and carries a regression instrument (soak corpus / unit test).

required.title: Fallback-degradation root fix + semantic turn classifier foundation
required.summary: Operator probing exposed a deterministic fallback-degradation class: a tool-grounded QUESTION gets tool_choice=auto (requiredActionEvidenceForTurnPolicy only forces required for artifact-write/web-read/memory-action, not scheduling/inspection/status questions); on auto a capable model calls the tool but a weak/non-thinking fallback (AI21 Jamba via openrouter) NARRATES (tool_calls=0). Full deterministic chain traced to tool_choice + a model that accepts required and ignores it (cloud-provider's reject_required_tool_choice is scoped to cloud-provider only; openrouter sent real required; Jamba ignored it). Reactive fixes (committed, unit-verified): force tool_choice=required (one-shot SetForceToolCall) when a guard retry catches narrate-instead-of-call (1b69dcb3); record circuit-breaker-open provider skips as fallback events so terminal failures can name the cause (9fc3c2bc); DON'T trust a model that ignores required — try next, fail loud with the honest cause (7cac9a7e); provider-degradation fail-loud message (cc55a75a). PROACTIVE root fix + anti-brittleness: a new embedding-centroid TurnClassifier (extends GuardSemanticClassifier) replaces phrase-list turn heuristics; first head REQUIRES_TOOL vs SELF_CONTAINED calibrated against the pinned embed-model embedder (held-out gap 0.038, F1 1.0, threshold 0.005) and WIRED so a tool-grounded turn proactively forces tool_choice=required from the opening inference (919b8867, slice 3) — no dependency on the variable task_deferral guard, generalizes across tools. DONE 2026-06-10: the remaining three phrase-list heads are migrated to the TurnClassifier, each calibrated against the pinned embed-model embedder and gated on its own soak. (head 2) topic-shift vs continuation (f3cbcdf9) breaks fresh-external web-read inheritance on a semantic subject change beyond the lexical signalsTopicChange markers — held-out shift.min +0.0100 vs continuation.max -0.0143, threshold 0.005, soak asserts no continuation crosses it (regression vector: a false shift drops web-read mid-correction). (head 3) intent action/question/social (54aa71b4) rescues classifyIntent's <50-char conversational + trailing-general fallthroughs so a marker-less imperative is not routed to social mode with zero tools — action cut 0.02, question cut -0.02 (margins vs best competitor), with an explicit Classified flag so an unclassified zero-margin never fires the rescue. (head 4) scheduling-vs-web (c72f2131) gives marker-less scheduler turns the focused scheduling envelope and wins the scheduling-vs-web tie the web-read case took by case order, except for explicit-URL turns — sched.min +0.0292 vs web.max -0.0149, threshold 0.02. Each head reuses one single per-turn embed (recorded on the session at decomposition); strong lexical results are never overridden; a zero classification degrades to lexical-only behavior. Adversarial gate 27/27 and full behavioral soak 38/38 (all grade A, release_gate_findings=0, retry_model_call_waits=0) on cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-flash, commit e72763d5. The soak also caught + fixed an unrelated web-evidence regression (e72763d5): Cloudflare fronting example.com added a single-token 'Allow: GET, HEAD' header that escaped the name-based transport filters and was projected verbatim as a web-read final answer; StripLeadingHTTPHeaderBlocks now drops leading HTTP header blocks positionally (redirect-chain aware, does not swallow header-adjacent bodies), with the standard single-token header names added as defense-in-depth and a verbatim-capture regression test.
required.rationale: Behavioral correctness on tool-grounded turns is paramount; the degradation produced narration/fabrication or generic give-ups when capable providers were down. The reactive chain makes the failure honest; the turn classifier makes it not happen (force the tool call up front) and is the systemic replacement for the brittle phrase-list classification the operator flagged. Each classifier head ships only if its calibration/soak number beats the heuristic it replaces.

required.title: Config hot-reload: watcher wired into the daemon with boot-path parity and event bridging
required.summary: Wired the previously-unwired core.ConfigWatcher into the daemon runtime (operator-directed 2026-06-10). The watcher polls flowforge.toml, validates, applies changes across registered component reloaders in order with batch rollback on any failure, and emits config lifecycle events. Wiring decisions: (1) reload parity with the boot path — reloads go through new core.LoadEffectiveConfigFromFile (file + provider-pack + bundled-provider merges) because plain LoadConfigFromFile would silently strip merge-supplied behavior flags such as reject_required_tool_choice, re-introducing the local tool-turn hard-fail the same day it was fixed; (2) Watch(ctx, bootCfg) seeds from the daemon's already-loaded viper config (env/flag overlays intact) and starts polling without re-reading or re-applying — boot overlays stay authoritative until the file actually changes; (3) lifecycle events are logged and bridged onto the api.EventBus dashboard/WS surface with a slim payload (status/fields/error, no config snapshots); (4) first reloader is log level (core.ParseLogLevel promoted from cmd/root.go's private copy), the canonical safe hot-reloadable setting; rollback works via the watcher's swapped-args re-apply. The poll goroutine exits with the daemon lifecycle ctx (no leak). Verified by running the real app from source against ~/.flowforge-dev watcher start logged, live edit of log_level info->debug applied within one poll cycle (file_detected -> log level applied -> config.changed applied, debug lines flowing), then debug->info applied on revert. Unit coverage: watcher reload/rollback/validation suite, Watch-seeds-without-initial-apply, effective-load bundled-behavior parity, logLevelReloader apply/rollback/no-op, nil-watcher-on-empty-path.
evidence: live dev-instance verification 2026-06-10: log_level info->debug->info hot-applied within one poll cycle, config lifecycle events logged

required.title: Partial triple-lens code audit: hot-path bug fixes (billing account nonce liveness, transactional session delete) + zero-TODO closure
required.summary: A partial triple-lens code audit (architectural mandates + clean-code + clean-architecture, operator-directed 2026-06-10) ran against the behavioral core and infrastructure hot paths mid-release. Mandate compliance: PASS — all nine critical rules verified (pipeline owns behavior, connectors parse/call/format only, single serialized money seam, authenticated/deduped ingress, no off-pipeline inference, authority via central policy), actively enforced by architecture_fitness_test.go, with only two LOW documentation nits (off-pipeline exemption registry omissions). Two real pre-existing bugs were found and fixed per the fix-all-findings rule (commit 1946890a): (1) billing account liveness — SendTransactionWithParamsContext optimistically advanced nextPendingNonce but never rolled it back on a build/sign error or transient broadcast failure; since the reset guard only re-syncs when chain is AHEAD of local, one failed send wedged the billing account permanently (every later tx signed a future nonce that never mines) until daemon restart; now the reservation is rolled back exactly under txMu unless the broadcast was accepted, with a regression test asserting no gap across repeated failures. (2) data integrity / fail-loud — SessionRepository.DeleteSession ran ~11 cascade deletes outside any transaction and discarded every intermediate error, so a store_snapshot mid-cascade could delete the parent session while orphaning tool_calls/turns/pipeline_traces; the cascade now runs in store.InTx with every error propagated, regression-tested incl. grandchild rows. Also closed the no-TODOs rule to a provable zero (9dc3e29c): the only TODO token in non-test source was a self-referential string literal in an exercise prompt, reworded so marker scans read true zero and the eval can no longer be gamed by echoing the prompt's own token. Structural findings (god files/functions, LoopDeps concrete-store testability gap) were roadmapped (decompose-behavioral-core-god-files), and the COMPREHENSIVE audit was deliberately parked as the opening activity of the next release (roadmap full-codebase-triple-lens-audit) — mid-stabilization is the wrong time for a tree-wide sweep.

planned.title: Remove foreign 'Architext 1.1.2' release + v1.1.2 tag from the flowforge GitHub repo
planned.summary: On 2026-05-16 an Architext release ceremony ran `gh release create` from a flowforge working directory (architext's justfile `github-release` recipe omits --repo, so gh resolved the target repo from the cwd remote), publishing release 'Architext 1.1.2' with lightweight tag v1.1.2 at flowforge commit fddc7e0f onto code-host.example.com/repo The identical release (same title and body) was published on example-org/architext 26 seconds later, so the flowforge copy is a contentless duplicate with zero assets. Impact: (1) it is marked Latest until the FlowForge v1.1.0 GitHub release publishes, so cmd/updatecmd/update.go (/releases/latest) and the legacy flowforge-rust updater resolve a foreign release today; (2) the v1.1.2 tag permanently blocks a future FlowForge v1.1.2 tag. Verified safe to delete (2026-06-11): the release has zero assets, GitHub-wide code search finds zero references to its URL, and npm installs of @example-org/architext come from the npm registry tarball, never GitHub releases. Resolution per operator decision 2026-06-11: delete the GitHub release, the remote v1.1.2 tag, and stale local-clone tags, and pin `--repo example-org/architext` in architext's justfile github-release recipe so the recurrence vector is closed.
planned.rationale: Not a v1.1.0 ship gate: publishing the FlowForge v1.1.0 release supersedes the foreign release as Latest regardless. Tracked here so the tag-namespace collision is cleared before any future v1.1.2 is planned and so updater /releases/latest hygiene never depends on a foreign artifact. Executed in a dedicated architext-repo session (operator decision) because the recurrence fix (justfile --repo pin) lives in that repo, not on this ceremony branch.
evidence: gh release view v1.1.2 -R example-org/flowforge (2026-06-11): zero assets, isImmutable=false, lightweight tag at fddc7e0f, published 2026-05-16T09:14:19Z, still badged Latest

GitHub code search 2026-06-11: 0 hits for 'flowforge/releases/tag/v1.1.2'; only asset-based updaters reference flowforge releases/download and the foreign release has no assets

blockers.title: Cross-platform bundled TTS publication proof
blockers.summary: Runtime discovery, installed voice-ID selection, installer/update/release workflow contracts, and packaged Piper asset handling passed. A 2026-06-02 pre-ceremony dry-run of the bundled-Piper publish step (new since v1.0.12, never run in a real release) found it would publish CRASH-ON-LAUNCH macOS voice runtimes: upstream rhasspy/piper 2023.11.14-2 macOS tarballs ship the piper binary but omit its @rpath runtime dylibs (libespeak-ng/libpiper_phonemize/libonnxruntime), and the binary carries no embedded rpath. Linux/Windows bundles were unaffected (they ship their .so/.dll beside the binary; all 5 upstream platform assets + the ModelHub voice model returned HTTP 200). Fixed in commit 2f5a91be: release.yml now vendors the three macOS dylibs from rhasspy/piper-phonemize 2023.11.14-4 (x86_64, matching both x86_64 darwin piper binaries) under the exact leaf names the @rpath load commands request (excluding the unloadable .dSYM companion), and internal/voice/piper.go launches piper with DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH pointed at the bundle bin dir on darwin. Proven end-to-end: a workflow-faithful assembly synthesized a valid WAV on darwin/arm64 (PIPER_EXIT=0). Final published-artifact verification remains a release-ceremony check, but the latent macOS publication defect is now closed pre-ceremony.
blockers.nextAction: During release ceremony, verify the published Piper assets are installable on supported platforms; macOS bundles now vendor piper-phonemize dylibs (commit 2f5a91be) and resolve them via DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH.
